results.question: For an AHU or VAV terminal, what does G36 say about low airflow alarms?

sample_matches.title: Low Airflow Alarm Suppression for Zero Importance Multiplier Zones
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If a zone has an importance multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

sample_matches.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm - Level 3
sample_matches.evidence_sample: b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Low Primary Airflow Alarm - Level 4
sample_matches.evidence_sample: a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Dual-Duct VAV Terminal Unit Low Airflow Alarm
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Low Airflow Alarm - 50% of Setpoint
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Low Airflow Alarm - 70% of Setpoint
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm.

sample_matches.title: Low Airflow Alarm Suppression for Importance-Multiplier 0
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone.

results.question: How are AHU SAT reset requests generated?

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.8.8.1.  Cooling SAT Reset Requests

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests - 0 Requests (Cooling Loop < 95%)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests - 1 Request (Cooling Loop > 95%)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests - 2 Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Requests - 3 Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Request - 0 Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cooling SAT Reset Request - 1 Request
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%.

results.question: How are duct static pressure reset requests generated?

sample_matches.title: Multiple-Zone VAV AHU – Static Pressure Setpoint Reset (T&R)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters shown in Table 5.16.1.2.

sample_matches.title: Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using T&R Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters shown in Table 5.16.1.2.

sample_matches.title: Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using Trim & Respond
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters shown in Table 5.16.1.2.

sample_matches.title: Dual-Fan Dual-Duct Heating VAV AHU Static Pressure Setpoint Reset
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

sample_matches.title: Static Pressure Setpoint Reset using T&R Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Static pressure setpoint. Setpoint shall be reset using T&R logic (see Section 5.1.14) using the parameters in Table 5.17.1.2.

sample_matches.title: Static Pressure Reset Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.

sample_matches.title: Static Pressure Reset Requests - 3 Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: a. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint while setpoint is greater than zero and the damper position is greater than 95% for 1 minute, send 3 requests.

sample_matches.title: Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Request - 0 Requests
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Else if the damper position is less than 95%, send 0 requests.

results.question: What AFDD rules and fault conditions are available for AHUs?

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#13
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC #13 Equation: SAT AVG > SAT SP-C + Ɛ SAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#3
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC #3 (omit if no MAT sensor) Equation: MAT AVG - ƐMAT > min[(RAT AVG + Ɛ RAT), (OAT AVG + Ɛ OAT)] Applies to OS #1 – #5 Description: MAT too high; should be between OAT and RAT Possible Diagnosis: RAT sensor error, MAT sensor error, OAT se

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#7
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC #7 (omit if no heating coil) Equation: SAT AVG < SATSP - ƐSAT and HC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #1 Description: SAT too low in full heating Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve leaking or stuck open, Heating coil valve stuck

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#1: Duct Static Pressure Too Low
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#1 Equation: DSP AVG < DSPSP - ƐDSP and VFDSPD ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD. Applies to OS #1 – #5. Description: Duct static pressure is too low with fan at full speed.

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#6: OA Fraction Error
sample_matches.evidence_sample: nevertheless occur if so determined by the fault condition tests in Section 5.16.14.8 .

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Condition FC#7: SAT Too Low in Full Heating
sample_matches.evidence_sample: nevertheless occur if so determined by the fault condition tests in Section 5.16.14.8 .

sample_matches.title: AFDD Fault Conditions for VAV AHU
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Table 5.16.14.8 shows potential fault conditions that can be evaluated by the AFDD routines. If the equation statement is TRUE, then the specified fault condition exists.

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#13: SAT Too High in Full Cooling
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#13 Equation: SAT AVG > SATSP + ƐSAT and CC ≥ 99% Applies to OS #3, #4 Description: SAT too high in full cooling Possible Diagnosis: SAT sensor error, Cooling coil valve stuck closed or actuator failure, Fouled or undersized cooling coil,

results.question: How should the chilled water plant be enabled and disabled?

sample_matches.title: Chiller Plant Enable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.20.2.2.  Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:

sample_matches.title: Chilled Water Plant Enable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.20.2.2. Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT>CH -LOT, and c. The chiller plant

sample_matches.title: Chiller Plant Disable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.

sample_matches.title: Chilled Water Plant Disable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.

results.question: What does G36 say about chiller stage up and stage down logic?

sample_matches.title: Stage Up Failsafe Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Failsafe Condition: i. Secondary CHW supply temperature > primary CHW supply temperature + 2°F for 10 minutes and the enabled primary pumps are at maximum speed; or ii. Primary CHW supply temperature is 2°F > setpoint for 15 minutes.

sample_matches.title: Chiller Stage Up – Availability Condition
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage are unavailable. The availability condition is not subject to the minimum stage runtime requirement.

sample_matches.title: Chiller Staging – Stage Down Condition
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage

sample_matches.title: Chiller Staging – Stage Up Efficiency Condition
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Efficiency Condition : Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes

sample_matches.title: Stage Down Condition (Primary-Only without WSE)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage

sample_matches.title: Stage Down Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage

sample_matches.title: Stage Up Efficiency Condition (Primary-Only without WSE)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Efficiency Condition: Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; or

sample_matches.title: Stage Up Efficiency Condition (Primary-Secondary without WSE)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Efficiency Condition: Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; or

results.question: What AFDD fault diagnostics are available for the chilled water plant?

sample_matches.title: AFDD – Chilled Water Supply Temperature Too High
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high

sample_matches.title: AFDD – Condenser Approach Too High
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#8 Equation Approach COND ≥ RefrigCondTemp CH-x, AVG - CWRT CH-x, AVG Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Condenser approach is too high

sample_matches.title: AFDD – Evaporator Approach Too High
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#9 Equation Approach EVAP ≥ CHWST CH-x, AVG - RefrigEvapTemp CH-x, AVG Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Evaporator approach is too high

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#14: CWST Too High with Towers at Full Speed
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#14 Equation CWST AVG - ƐCWT ≥ DesCWSTdes and SpeedCT ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Condenser water supply temperature is too high with cooling tower(s) at full speed.

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#1: DP Too High with Pumps Off
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#1 Equation DP AVG > Ɛ DSP and Status CHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Differential pressure is too high with the chilled water pumps off Possible Diagnosis DP sensor error

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#2: Primary Flow Too High with Pumps Off
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#2 Equation FLOW P, AVG > Ɛ FM and Status PCHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Primary chilled water flow is too high with the chilled water pumps off Possible Diagnosis Flow meter error

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#4: DP Too Low with Pumps at Full Speed
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#4 Equation DP AVG < DPSP – ƐDP and Speed CHWP ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water loop differential pressure is too low with chilled water pump(s) at full speed.

sample_matches.title: AFDD FC#5: Primary Flow Too Low with Bypass Fully Open
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#5 Equation FLOW P, AVG < CHW-MinFlowSp – ƐFM and MFBPV ≥ 99% - ƐMFBPV Applies to OS #2, #3, #5 Description Primary chilled water flow is too low with the minimum flow bypass valve fully open.

results.question: How should the hot water plant or boiler plant be enabled and disabled?

sample_matches.title: Boiler Plant Enable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.21.2.2.  Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:

sample_matches.title: Hot Water Plant Enable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.21.2.2. Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot -Water Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, ad justable), and b. OAT<HW -LOT, and c. The Bo

sample_matches.title: Plant Enable/Disable - Enable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: 5.21.2.2.  Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and:

sample_matches.title: Boiler Plant Disable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT>HW-LOT + 1°F, or c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.

sample_matches.title: Hot Water Plant Disable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT>HW-LOT + 1°F, or c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.

sample_matches.title: Plant Enable/Disable - Disable Logic
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Heating Hot-Water Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT>HW-LOT + 1°F, or c. The Boiler plant enable schedule is inactive.

results.question: What does G36 say about boiler stage up and stage down logic?

sample_matches.title: Boiler Minimum Stage Runtime
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Each stage shall have a minimum runtime of 10 minutes.

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Primary Only Condensing: Stage Down
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail s

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Variable Primary/Variable Secondary Condensing: Stage Up
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage up if any of the following is true: 1. Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage is unavailable. The availability condition is not subject to the minimum stage runtime requirement. Or 2. Efficiency C

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Non-Condensing: Stage Down
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next lower available stage for 10 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Hybrid: Stage Up to Condensing Stage
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If all boilers enabled in the next higher stage are condensing, stage up if any of the following is true: 1. Availability Condition: The equipment necessary to operate the current stage is unavailable. The availability condition is not subj

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Hybrid: Stage Down from Condensing Stage
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If all boilers enabled in the current stage are condensing, stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. For 5 minutes, Primary HW pumps are

sample_matches.title: Boiler Staging - Hybrid: Stage Down from Non-Condensing Stage
sample_matches.evidence_sample: If any boiler enabled in the current stage is non-condensing, stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity of the next available lower stage for 10 minutes. 2. The failsafe stage up condi

sample_matches.title: Boiler Stage Down – Efficiency Condition (Condensing)
sample_matches.evidence_sample: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail s

results.question: What hot water plant AFDD fault conditions are available?

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#10: Supply Temperature Sensor Deviation
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#10 Equation | (∑(HW-Flow B-X * HWST B-X) / ∑HW-Flow B-X) - HWST AVG | > ƐHWT Applies to OS #2 Description Deviation between the active boiler hot water supply temperature and the common hot water supply temperature is too high.

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#11: Return Temperature Sensor Deviation
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#11 Equation | (∑(HW-Flow B-X * HWRT B-X) / ∑HW-Flow B-X) - HWRT AVG | > ƐHWT Applies to OS #2 Description Deviation between the active boiler hot water return temperature and the common boiler entering water temperature is too high.

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#1: DP Too High with Pumps Off
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#1 Equation DP AVG > Ɛ DSP and Status HWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Differential pressure is too high with the hot water pumps off Possible Diagnosis DP sensor error

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#2: Primary Flow Too High with Pumps Off
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#2 Equation FLOW P, AVG > Ɛ FM and Status PHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Primary hot water flow is too high with the hot water pumps off Possible Diagnosis Flow meter error

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#3: Secondary Flow Too High with Pumps Off
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#3 Equation FLOW S, AVG > Ɛ FM and Status SHWP = Off Applies to OS #1 Description Secondary hot water flow is too high with the associated hot water pumps off Possible Diagnosis Flow meter error

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#4: DP Too Low with Pumps at Full Speed
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#4 Equation DP AVG < DPSP – ƐDP and Speed HWP ≥ 99% - ƐVFDSPD Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water loop differential pressure is too low with hot water pump(s) at full speed.

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#5: Primary Flow Too Low with Bypass Fully Open
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#5 Equation FLOW P, AVG < HW-MinFlowSp – ƐFM and MFBPV ≥ 99% - ƐMFBPV Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Primary hot water flow is too low with the minimum flow bypass valve fully open.

sample_matches.title: AFDD - FC#6: HW Supply Temperature Too Low
sample_matches.evidence_sample: FC#6 Equation HWST AVG + Ɛ HWT < HWSTSP Applies to OS #2, #3 Description Hot water supply temperature is too low.
